title: thelasis pygmaea var. khasiana
intro: Thelasis pygmaea var. khasiana is a species of orchid native to the Himalayas. It is a type of herb used in traditional medicine, known for its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including relieving fever, reducing pain and inflammation, and treating respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. In herbal preparations, Thelasis pygmaea var. khasiana is typically used to make teas, infusions, and extracts, which are consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
